note: Public receipts ledger. Every entry is committed before the outcome is known; graded entries record the pre-registered probability, the outcome, hit/miss on the modal call, and the Brier score (lower is better; 0.25 = coin flip). This file is the source of truth for the website receipts page.

validation.description: v2 model frozen on pre-June data, scored on 884 unseen games (2026-06-01 through 2026-07-11).

entries.title: MLP Mid-Season Tournament Gold Final — New Jersey 5s vs St. Louis Shock
entries.outcome_summary: St. Louis Shock won 3-0. MXD2 skipped as a dead game; no DreamBreaker. Anna Leigh Waters lost both her games (6-11 WD, 8-11 MXD1).

items.label: Overall: St. Louis Shock wins the final

entries.footnote: The earlier v1 forecast (same file, committed hours before) grades identically in direction: overall STL 57.0% HIT, WD 69.5% MISS, MD 79.1% HIT, MXD1 52.9% MISS. Both models called the match and missed the headline WD game.

entries.title: Preregistered chemistry predictions (second half of 2026 season)
entries.model: v1 dyad chemistry + fixed-effects regression scoring (method frozen in the source file)

items.label: Waters + Bright (womens, PPA) underperform sum-of-parts in remaining 2026 games

entries.footnote: Scored in September against games dated after 2026-07-12 only, using the method written into the frozen file.

entries.title: MLP San Diego day 1 — five-matchup slate vs the frozen site forecasts
entries.source: data/forecasts.json (generated 2026-07-14, committed to main before the event)
entries.model: v2 current values, projected lineups, singles-model DreamBreakers, calibrated
entries.outcome_summary: Favorites went 4/5. Columbus swept both its matchups 4-0; SoCal and Phoenix won as modest favorites; the miss was Phoenix-Orlando, where 61% Phoenix lost 2-3 on a DreamBreaker the model had priced at 37%. Jack Sock (in for the projected Yates Johnson) played both Orlando matchups.

items.label: Matchup 1: Columbus Sliders over Orlando Squeeze
Matchup 2: SoCal Hard Eights over Florida Smash
Matchup 3: Phoenix Flames over Orlando Squeeze
Matchup 4: Columbus Sliders over California Black Bears
Matchup 5: Phoenix Flames over Florida Smash

entries.footnote: Matchup mean Brier 0.130 (coin flip = 0.25). Game level, matched on the forecast's own team-one pairing: 12/13 hits, mean Brier 0.092, two modal scores exact (both 11-6 mixed calls); 7 games VOID where the projected pairing never took the court, and opponent-side substitutions (Sock for Johnson, twice) graded through as lineup risk. Reaches-a-DreamBreaker graded 4/5 on the modal call, mean Brier 0.136 — the miss was the 37.4% DB that decided Phoenix-Orlando. POST-HOC COUNTERFACTUAL (not a regrade; committed grades stand): repricing the four Sock-affected games with actual lineups (Sock 0.837 vs Johnson 0.369 on 448/303 games) flips the matchup-3 call to Orlando 68% (Brier 0.377 → 0.102) and dissolves the MD game miss (0.316 → 0.023) — the day's headline miss was lineup information, not skill values. It cuts the other way too: Columbus's wins over the Sock-priced pairs (11-6, 11-1) become 48% and 90% calls, so the affected games' Brier sum rises 0.362 → 0.507. The model can't take credit both ways; the ledger keeps the frozen numbers.
